rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: Minor documentation omission: the memo does not explicitly state the divisibility model (integer vs continuous) for allocations; while the allocation uses fractional/splitting logic implicitly and marks missing route-level inputs as needs_review, the divisibility assumption is not stated explicitly as required by the rubric. No numeric inputs were fabricated and no capacity constraints were exceeded.
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Add an explicit divisibility statement in path_memo.md (e.g., 'We assume continuous/divisible allocations across resources' or 'We require integer, vehicle-level allocations - see needs_review') and discuss how the integer-vs-continuous choice would affect feasibility or cost. Optionally attach solver logs or an LP formulation and solver output to certify optimality under the chosen model; include a short certificate (dual bound or solver status) for stronger confidence.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: All required artifacts present. Numeric inputs in path_memo.md and coverage_assignment.json match the packet citations (R1=44@20, R2=61@26, R3=39@16; D1=32, D2=49, D3=27). The allocation plan exactly satisfies each demand (D1=32, D2=49, D3=27) and respects resource capacities (R3 used 39/39, R1 used 44/44, R2 used 25/61). The candidate explicitly documents the interpretation that costs are fixed activation costs, correctly computes total fixed cost = 20+26+16 = 62, and provides pairwise capacity checks that show all three resources are required under that interpretation. Validation checks and allocation tables are provided and reproducible from the cited packet values.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: For stronger verification and to reach full verifier-grade confidence, include a formal LP/assignment formulation and solver output (solver log or certificate) or an explicit short proof of optimality; also, if available, include a demand-to-resource compatibility matrix or explain why none is needed. Attach the full coverage_assignment.json (not only preview) and any solver inputs/outputs for reproducibility.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: All required artifacts are present. The path_memo.md explicitly cites input/task.json lines and reproduces the numeric inputs (R1=44@20, R2=61@26, R3=39@16; D1=32, D2=49, D3=27). Allocations respect resource capacities (R1 used 44 <=44, R2 used 25 <=61, R3 used 39 <=39) and satisfy all demands (each demand met). Total capacity (144) > total demand (108), slack computed (36) matches. Per-resource costs and the aggregated total cost (2154) are recomputed and match the artifact. The memo states the divisibility assumption (demands may be split) and marks missing compatibility/route inputs as needs_review. The cheapest-capacity-first method is stated and, given the explicit linear per-unit costs and pooled-capacity assumption, is cost-optimal; documentation provides intermediate calculations allowing exact reproduction. No fabricated numeric inputs were detected; missing fields were marked needs_review as required by the rubric.
